title: Remote connect-readiness gate + cancel a not-started queued job
body:
TWO related remote-engine UX bugs found live 2026-06-13 during MPI-64 testing (branch RunPod). Connect to B4 part 2/4 (readiness/reconnect state).

## Bug 1 — premature 'Connected' before the Pod WS is ready
On boot auto-reconnect (and possibly Settings Connect), the app shows a 'Connected' popup / IDLE·REMOTE while the Pod is still RESUMING and the binary-preview WebSocket is NOT yet up. Live evidence: app restarted 07:29 → boot reconnect 07:30:00 → `Pod resume kicked off` 07:30:03 → `WebSocket error (may be transient)` 07:30:27; console: `WebSocket connection to wss://igcx9udiw5471i-8889.proxy.runpod.net/ws... failed`. The user, trusting 'Connected', queued 2 I2V jobs into the CUE → the RUNNING job hung in STARTING because the WS never connected → nothing actually generating on the Pod (telemetry VRAM 1% / GPU 0%). This is a worse form of the already-logged stale-connection-feed bug.
FIX DIRECTION: gate the 'Connected'/ready signal on the binary-preview WS actually being open (or `/remote/comfy/status` ready AND a successful WS handshake), not just the Pod resume kickoff. Until truly ready, show 'connecting…' and ideally block/queue-guard generation so jobs aren't accepted against a not-ready engine.

## Bug 2 — Stop/Cancel can't clear a job stuck in STARTING (no prompt_id yet)
When a CUE job is in STARTING (it never received a prompt_id because the WS never connected), pressing STOP is a no-op: `exec.cancel()` → `comfyController.interrupt()` POSTs `/proxy/interrupt`, but there is no running ComfyUI prompt to interrupt → the renderer's job state never clears → the queue panel hangs (1 RUNNING / 1 QUEUED), multiple STOP presses do nothing.
FIX DIRECTION: a client-side cancel path that clears a STARTING/queued CUE job WITHOUT depending on the WS / a ComfyUI prompt_id — if no prompt_id has been assigned, just end the generation locally (activeGenerations.end + clear the Cue job) instead of (or in addition to) the no-op interrupt. NOTE: a SINGLE healthy gen's Stop DOES work (verified this session, ~5s + Pod VRAM drops) — this bug is specific to a job that never started on the Pod.

## Repro
1. Remote mode, Pod stopped/resuming. Restart app → boot auto-reconnect.
2. As soon as 'Connected' shows (before the WS is truly up), queue 1-2 I2V jobs.
3. Running job hangs in STARTING; STOP does nothing.

## Verify
- 'Connected' only appears once the preview WS is actually open; generation is not accepted before then.
- STOP on a STARTING/queued job clears it immediately (queue empties), even with no live WS.

Found during MPI-64 (RunPod Remote Engine). Distinct from the backend stream-pipe crash (fixed dcd3482) and from B0/B4 part 1+3 (committed).
